question_title: How many commercial aircraft deliveries will Airbus report for February 2026 ?
summary: Airbus February 2026 delivery forecast based on historical patterns, current supply chain conditions, and scenario analysis. January 2026 was only 19 deliveries (slowest start in a decade), driven by A320 fuselage panel quality issues and persistent supply chain constraints. February historically rebounds significantly from January (mean increase of ~20 units), but current conditions suggest the recovery may be muted. Three-scenario Monte Carlo model: Upside (30%, ~47 mean, inspections clearing), Base (55%, ~38 mean, moderate recovery), Downside (15%, ~28 mean, continued severe constraints). Central estimate ~39 deliveries.

factors.description: January 2026 was only 19 deliveries - slowest start in a decade, indicating severe current supply constraints
February ALWAYS rebounds significantly from January - historical mean increase of ~20 units (range 10-26)
A320 fuselage panel quality issue 'rectified' per Airbus Dec 2025, but inspections of existing aircraft continue
Historical Feb deliveries post-COVID: 40-49 range (2022-2025), suggesting floor around 40 in normal conditions
BofA estimated 10-15 aircraft held up by inspections - potential for batch release boosting February
Airbus has NOT released 2026 delivery guidance - unusual for Feb, signals management uncertainty
Persistent supply chain issues: engines, cabin interiors, seating shortages remain structural headwinds
Airbus needs to ramp toward ~900 annual deliveries - internal pressure to accelerate from Jan lows

Now let me save findings and write the meta-reflection.## Forecast Summary: Airbus February 2026 Deliveries

**Central estimate: ~39 aircraft** (median of distribution)

### Key Evidence
- **January 2026 was only 19 deliveries** — slowest start in a decade, driven by A320 panel inspections and supply chain issues
- **February always rebounds from January** — historical increase of 10-26 units, never less
- **Recent February values: 40-49** (2022-2025), but 2026 conditions suggest below-average outcome
- **Panel issue "rectified"** for new production, but inspection backlog continues
- **BofA estimates 10-15 aircraft held up** — potential for release boosting February

### Distribution (Percentiles)
| Percentile | Estimate |
|-----------|----------|
| P10 | 29 |
| P20 | 34 |
| P40 | 38 |
| P60 | 40 |
| P80 | 45 |
| P90 | 49 |

The distribution is wider than typical because of genuine scenario uncertainty: if inspection-delayed aircraft get released, February could reach the mid-40s; if supply constraints persist at January levels, it could stay in the high 20s to low 30s. The base case (~38, 55% weight) reflects moderate recovery with ongoing constraints.

resolution_reason: Official Airbus data confirms 35 commercial aircraft deliveries to 21 customers in February 2026. This is verified by the primary source (Airbus official Orders & Deliveries page) and corroborated by multiple credible aviation news sources.
resolution_criteria:
This question resolves as the total number of Airbus commercial aircraft “deliveries” for February 2026, using Airbus’ official Orders & Deliveries data:
Primary source: https://www.airbus.com/en/products-services/commercial-aircraft/market/orders-and-deliveries
Backup source: Airbus IR “Orders & deliveries” / monthly releases (if the above page structure changes). Question may also resolve based on  [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
background_info:
Airbus deliveries are a leading indicator for aerospace revenue recognition, supply-chain throughput, and airline fleet demand. Primary source: Airbus 'Orders and Deliveries' reports page (monthly reports that can be summed for Jan–Mar 2026), https://www.airbus.com/en/products-services/commercial-aircraft/orders-and-deliveries.
